revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Jingus without interwikis]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24593703458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izanagi Jingū
伊弉諾神宮 | image = Izanagi-jingu, haiden-1.jpg | caption = Izanagi Jingū Haiden {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|27|36|N|134|51|08|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Izanagi]], {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location =740 Taga, Awaji-shi, Hyōgo-ken 656-1521 | website = {{Official|1=https://izanagi-jingu.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = April 22 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Izanagi Jingū'''|伊弉諾神宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Taga neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Awaji, Hyōgo|en|Awaji, Hyōgo|qid=Q373107|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|qid=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on April 22. {{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Izanagi Jingū are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Izanagi|Izanagi-no-mikoto]]'''| 伊弉諾尊 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-mikoto|1=Izanami}}'''| 伊弉冉尊 |}} ==History== Per [[Japanese mythology]] as outlined in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', the [[Japanese archipelago]] (starting with {{ill|Awaji Island|en|Awaji Island|qid=Q504373|lt=Awaji Island|1=Awaji Island}}), the ''[[kami]]'', and all living things were created by the progenitor gods Izanagi and Izanami. After this work was completed, Izanagi retired into seclusion on Awaji Island where his tomb was located on the site of his palace. The documentary history, mention of a shrine to Izanagi in Awaji is made in an entry in the ''Nihon Shoki'' under the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Richu|Emperor Richu]] and {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|qid=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} and the shrine appears in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' in an entry dated 859. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'', it is listed a ''Myōjin Taisha'', and as the ''ichinomiya'' of the province. According to the records of nearby Myokyo-ji, Tamura Nakazane, a descendant of [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], rebuilt the shrine in 1280. The Tamura clan served as hereditary ''[[kannushi]]'' and as local feudal lords into the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}. In 1581, {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} ordered Tamura Tsuneharu to take the lead in a battle against [[Takeda Katsuyori]], but he was defeated and the Tamura clan were destroyed. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was supported by the [[:en:Hachitsuka clan|Hachitsuka clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|en|Tokushima Domain|qid=Q3034557|lt=Tokushima Domain|1=Tokushima Domain}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 2nd Rank |国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871 under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. It was promoted to an {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 1st Rank |官幣大社|Kanpei Taisha}} in 1885. Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. In 1932, Izanami was added to the roster of the shrine, which in 1954 was renamed from Izanagi Jinja to Izanagi Jingū.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The ''[[Honden]]'' is a three-bay ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]''-style building connected to the [[Heiden (Shinto)|Heiden]] by a roof. It was built on the site of the alleged tomb of Izanagi in 1882. The site had previously been a "forbidden land" upon which trespass was strictly forbidden for centuries. The Zuishin-mon on the approach to the shrine was completed in 1883. The Kamiike pond in the precincts is said to be the remains of a moat which once surrounded the ruins of Izanagi's palace and tomb. {{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} == Gallery == File:Izanagi-jingu, honden.jpg|Honden File:Izanagi-jingu, haraeden.jpg|Haraeden File:Izanagi-jingu, shinmon.jpg|Zuishin-mon File:Houjou no Shinchi.JPG|Kamiike File:Izanagi-jingu, torii.jpg|Torii . ==See also== *{{ill|List of Jingū|en|List of Jingū|qid=Q120680118|lt=List of Jingū|1=List of Jingū}} *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Izanagi-jingu}} *[https://izanagi-jingu.jp Official homepage] *[https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6328110.html Hyōgo Jinja Honcho] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Contemporary Jingu}} {{Izanagi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Awaji Province]] [[Category:Awaji, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Jingū]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] }} [[en:Izanagi Jingu]] [[en:Izanagi Jingu]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]]
